name: Strongly Connected Components

x.io: A directed graph → its partition into maximal sets where every vertex reaches every other.
correctness: Each component is maximal and mutually reachable
Condensation of components is a DAG
x.stability: Deterministic given a fixed adjacency order.
x.parallelisable: Tarjan/Kosaraju are serial DFS; parallel SCC (e.g. Forward-Backward) exists for large graphs.

def: Linear-time algorithms (Tarjan, Kosaraju) that partition a directed graph into maximal mutually-reachable components.
problem: Find cycles / mutual reachability in a directed graph and collapse them into a DAG for further analysis.
mechanism: Tarjan runs one DFS tracking discovery indices and low-link values, popping a component when a root is found. Kosaraju does two passes (DFS, then DFS on the transposed graph in finish-time order).
benefits: Yields the condensation DAG for downstream ordering
limits: Tarjan recursion can overflow the stack on deep graphs
Two-pass Kosaraju needs the transpose graph
failures: Recursive DFS stack overflow on huge graphs (use explicit stack)
whenNot: Only cycle existence needed (a simpler DFS suffices)
myths: "SCC needs all-pairs reachability" — it is linear, not quadratic
